Home / Fortnite / Cosmetics / Battle Pass Cosmetics / Emotes

Emotes

Overview

As of May 29th 2026, there are around 2,036[1][2] Emotes In-Game

Emotes are Cosmetic Items introduced in Season 1. They can be used in Battle Royale, Save the World, Creative, Festival and LEGO Fortnite.

Additional features available for Emotes

Additional features available for Emotes[]
Built-In Traversal Group Dance
  • Glitter-up was the first built-in Emote.
  • Conga was the first traversal Emote.
  • High Five was the first group Emote.

Unreleased

Unreleased[]

Main Article: Unreleased Cosmetics#Emotes
TBD

TBD[]

Main Article: TBD Cosmetics#Emotes
Removed

Removed []

Main Article: Removed Cosmetics#Emotes
Trivia

Trivia[]

  • Fortnite has been sued several times over its Emotes since their introduction. These lawsuits might have prompted the creation of the Icon Series rarity, which is mostly composed of collaborations with choreographers who get paid and (occasionally) credited for their Emotes.[3]
    • A document from the 2024 Disney megaleak shows that Epic Games asks licensors to verify their ownership of the Emotes before they are added.[4] Thus, lawsuits such as the Bye Bye Bye one might have been the result of licensors incorrectly claiming their ownership of the Emotes.
References

References[]

  1. ↑ Includes TBD, Sidekick Emotes and Unreleased Emotes. Doesn't include Removed Emotes.
  2. ↑ Some Emotes (mostly synced) have more than 1 version which increases the number.
  3. ↑ I'm a FORTNITE EMOTE!! DID I GET PAID?? | Adam Rose.
  4. ↑ https://archive.org/details/f-063-hr-56-ku-4-fireplace-28.00-gtm-for-partner-10-5/page/n6/mode/1up
